text:
Garcia (ガルシア, Garushia) is a character that appears in The Last Mission movie. He was seen as one of the VIPs in the top box in Heavens Arena. Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission 

Appearance

Garcia was an older man with facial wrinkles, small black eyes, and gray hair that faded into light grey at the bottom. His thick eyebrows and Van Dyke beard were also grey. 

He wore a cedar-colored suit with gold buttons and cufflinks, a dark brown and yellow pinstriped tie, dark brown shoes, and a white dress shirt. In old black and white photos of him, he wore a similar, if not identical, outfit. 

Personality

Garcia is portrayed as a skeezy, money-focused person with little regard for the well-being of others. 

He's shown trying to rub elbows with affluent people like Neon, although she seems to be well aware of Garcia's insincere nature. When his life is threatened, he offers Jed a large sum of money to try and make the problem disappear without actually taking responsibility for his actions. 

Background

It's unclear who exactly Garcia worked for or what his job title was, but he most likely worked for the Hunter Association itself or the government in a high-up position. He's shown to have made decisions for the nation, given missions to Hunters, and was considered a VIP during the Battle Olympia event. 

Garcia was directly responsible for hiring Hunters to carry out a secret mission that involved putting the descendants of the Shadow into Kanan Prison Camp. All of them were primarily described as innocent people forced to revolt due to pressure from the government. This was done due to Garcia and the nation not wanting descendants of the Shadow to exist at all anymore. 

Old photographs depict Garcia with Pariston. It's implied that Pariston was either one of the Hunters hired by Garcia to execute the secret mission involving the descendants of the Shadow or that Pariston may have worked alongside Garcia in enforcing this mission. 

Plot

The Last Mission

Garcia approaches Neon in her VIP box before the beginning of the Battle Olympia tournament at Heavens Arena. He offers his sympathies about her father, who has recently fallen ill, but quickly gets dismissed by her, much to his annoyance. After he turns to walk away, Neon makes a face at him behind his back. 

After Jed and his followers force the Heavens Arena into a lockdown and take the spectators inside hostage, Garcia gets surrounded by Shadow Hunters and tries to argue for his innocence. 

Garcia is then brought up to the roof, where he tries to bribe Jed into letting him go. Instead, Jed tells Garcia to beg Netero for his life, claiming Netero is the only one who can help him now. Jed throws Garcia in front of Rengoku, whose On ability lifts him into the air, bringing him face to face with Netero while strangling him. 

During his interrogation, Garcia reveals that he was responsible for sending the Hunters to capture the descendants of the Shadow and put them into a prison camp. After getting the information they need out of him, Rengoku's On ability continues to strangle Garcia until eventually snapping his neck and lets Garcia's corpse fall onto the rooftop.
